'''Jedi sniper''' was a term used by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to describe one of three Jedi-held ranks in the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] [[Temple Security Force|Security Force]]. Alongside the [[Jedi Gate Master|Gate Master]] and the [[Jedi brute|brutes]], snipers were the Temple's first line of defense from troublemakers, zealots, and rioters.

==Overview==
[[File:JediSniper.jpg|thumb|left|A Jedi Sniper wielding a blue Lightsaber]]
When a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] began their independent studies separate from their former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], they had the option of choosing from three classes of Jedi: the [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Guardian]], [[Jedi Consular|Consular]], and [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Sentinel]]. A sniper of the Jedi Temple Security Force was a Guardian who pursued the specialization of [[Jedi Peacekeeper|Peacekeeper]] based on Coruscant.<ref name="TJP" />

Stationed on the parapets around the [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]], Temple snipers excelled at using their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] as ranged weapons and used the Force to enhance their vision as well as to help guide their long-range shots. Perfecting their [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic]] lightsaber forms, snipers were also highly acrobatic, capable of jumping, flipping, and running without the direct aid of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to propel themselves.<ref name="TJP" />

==History==
The rank of Temple snipers dates as far back as the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] and the reorganization of the Jedi Order by [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Fae Coven]]. Codifying the ranks and duties of the Jedi in her handbook called ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'', the term was used up until the dissolution of the Order in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. When the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was revealed to be a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he quickly ordered the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] to turn on the Jedi before they could remove the Sith from office. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' new apprentice led the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] against the Temple conducting [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]] as part of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. The snipers of the Temple defended it valiantly against the Sith and his army but were vastly outnumbered. By the end of the night, not a single sniper had survived the order. There were a total of 200 Jedi snipers on the day the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] fell.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith video game">[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]]</ref>
